spec_plan.title: Comprehensive Per-Module Test Coverage for Mamba Compiler and Runtime
spec_plan.summary: This reference context gathers specifications for comprehensive per-module test coverage across the Mamba Python compiler and runtime. The goal is to raise branch coverage from partial (0%-78%) to 100% for all 23 target modules across 4 batches. Coverage spans: lexer (tokenization, INDENT/DEDENT), parser (all expression/statement/pattern types), type checker (type narrowing), HIR/MIR lowering (desugaring, decision trees), codegen (Cranelift JIT/AOT), driver (module graph, config), runtime (thread safety, GC, async), FFI (C types, conversions), and 19 stdlib modules.

in_scope: Lexer (lexer/token.rs, 70%): INDENT/DEDENT handling, f-string tokenization, all token types
Parser (50-78%): all AST nodes, expressions (precedence, operators), statements, compound statements, patterns (literal, capture, sequence, mapping, class, OR, AS, guard)
Type checker (60-80%): bidirectional inference, type narrowing in case branches, protocol checking, generic constraints
HIR lowering (60-80%): AST desugaring rules, bare annotations, comprehensions, walrus operator, f-strings
MIR lowering (60-80%): decision tree compilation for patterns, nested pattern handling, comprehension lowering
Codegen (40-67%): Cranelift IR emission (all types, patterns, instructions), value marshaling, JIT symbol wiring, AOT object file linking
Driver (33-76%): mamba.toml discovery, module graph building, config file walking, entry point handling
Runtime value system (20-70%): NaN-boxed representation, type conversions, size/alignment assertions
Runtime GC (20-70%): mark phase, sweep phase, cycle detection, safepoint-based STW

Runtime concurrency (20-70%): atomic reference counting, per-object locking, thread lifecycle, synchronization primitives (barriers, channels, locks), global async state (DashMap/RwLock), Tokio integration, task spawning
FFI (0-50%): C type parsing, type mapping (CType enum), size/alignment checks, foreign function calls

Stdlib modules (0-49% coverage, 19 modules): argparse (20%), database/sqlite3 (49%), logging (46%), testing/unittest (31%), io (30%), struct/pickle (28%), diagnostics/traceback (25%), os (25%), network/socket (15%), concurrency/threading (10%), builtins (varies), and others
Integration tests: multi-file compilation, cross-module type checking, fixture-based scenarios, CPython conformance, deterministic concurrency testing
out_of_scope: Performance benchmarking (covered by separate benchmark suite)
Full conformance regression suite (covered by separate conformance tests)

sections.name: lexer-coverage
sections.reasoning: Defines tokenization, INDENT/DEDENT stack, f-string lexing, all token types. lexer/token.rs at 70% coverage (Batch C). Requires tests for all token types, indent/dedent transitions, f-string nesting, and edge cases.

sections.name: parser-coverage
sections.reasoning: AST node definitions, expression/statement/pattern parsing. Parser files at 50-78% coverage (Batch B-C). Requires unit tests for all AST variants, operator precedence, statement nesting, compound statements (if/for/while/with/try), and all pattern types.

sections.name: type-checking-coverage
sections.reasoning: Type inference, bidirectional checking, type narrowing in case branches. Type checker modules at 60-80% coverage. Requires tests for all type combinations, narrowing rules, generic constraints, and protocol satisfaction.

sections.name: lowering-coverage
sections.reasoning: AST desugaring to HIR, HIR to MIR with decision trees. Lowering modules at 60-80% coverage (Batch C). Requires tests for all desugaring rules (comprehensions, walrus, f-strings, bare annotations), pattern decision tree generation, and nested structures.

sections.name: codegen-coverage
sections.reasoning: Cranelift backend (core, JIT, AOT), value marshaling, instruction emission. Codegen modules at 40-67% coverage (Batch B-C). Requires tests for all instruction types, value conversions, type-specific paths, JIT symbol linking, and AOT object file generation.

sections.name: driver-coverage
sections.reasoning: Compiler driver: mamba.toml discovery, module graph, config parsing, backend selection. Driver modules at 33-76% coverage (Batch B-C). Requires tests for config file walking, module graph building, entry point handling, cross-module type checking.

sections.name: runtime-coverage
sections.reasoning: Runtime value system (NaN-boxing, RC), GC (mark/sweep, cycle detection), class system (MRO, dispatch, __init_subclass__), exception hierarchy. Runtime modules at 20-70% coverage (Batch B-C). Requires tests for all type conversions, RC edge cases, GC cycles, MRO resolution, and exception matching.

sections.name: concurrency-coverage
sections.reasoning: Thread safety (atomic RC, locks, safepoints), GC safepoints, async/await, Tokio integration, threading/subprocess stdlib. Critical integration tests required. Pre-clarifications Q2-Q3 specify real loopback sockets, deterministic synchronization, all thread lifecycle branches, synchronization primitives (barriers, channels).

sections.name: ffi-coverage
sections.reasoning: C type parsing, CType enum, type mapping, size/alignment assertions. FFI modules at 0-50% coverage (Batch A-B). Requires unit tests for all CType variants, type conversions, size assertions, alignment checks (per pre-clarification Q4).

sections.name: stdlib-coverage
sections.reasoning: 19 stdlib modules spanning 0-49% coverage (Batch A-B). Each spec defines function signatures, requirements, and test scenarios. Examples: argparse_mod.rs (20% → 100%), sqlite3_mod.rs (49% → 100%), logging_mod.rs (46% → 100%), unittest_mod.rs (31% → 100%). Tests require both unit (#[cfg(test)] inline) and integration forms, with no mocks, real loopback sockets for network tests.

sections.name: integration-test-patterns
sections.reasoning: Fixture test harness, directive dispatch, error reporting. Defines how integration tests are structured, executed, and verified (refs to pre-clarifications Q5-Q7). Patterns apply to multi-file compilation, fixture-based conformance, and CPython integration.

sections.name: stdlib-test-patterns
sections.reasoning: Completed coverage for 10 stdlib modules (queue, statistics, shlex, calendar, locale, lzma, zlib, secrets, bisect, abc); provides test pattern and infrastructure reuse. Shows concrete branch coverage requirements and concurrency/integration scenarios (e.g., thread-safe queue, concurrent put/get).

sections.name: context-requirements
sections.reasoning: Background context on P0/P1 features, language blockers, and registry/config system. Not directly specifying test code, but provide requirements for end-to-end verification (bare annotations, __init_subclass__, metaclass+generics, match/case XFAIL cleanup, config discovery).

metadata.notes: Final revision after two reviews. All 37 specs include requirement ID mappings. Added stdlib/testing.md for unittest_mod.rs (31%). Corrected relevance: high for per-module specs, medium for test infrastructure, low for background context. Organized into 12 focused sections covering all 23 target files across 4 batches.
